prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='svr', seasonality_champion='ONLY_FT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The true series exhibits mixed trend directions (up/down/up) with strong seasonal patterns. Models show extreme disagreement in seasonality capture (variance 0.942) and high overall model disagreement. The data strongly supports DBA combination with mixed weighting to leverage both the trend-champion svr and seasonality-champion ONLY_FT_catboost while accounting for significant model diversity.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_FT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "NaiveSeasonal", "late": "rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["rf", "svr", "ONLY_FT_svr", "CWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "FT_svr", "catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ARIMA", "NaiveMovingAverage", "NaiveSeasonal", "DWT_catboost", "ETS", "THETA"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.9073,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The true series exhibits mixed trend directions (up/down/up) with strong seasonal patterns. Models show extreme disagreement in seasonality capture (variance 0.942) and high overall model disagreement. The data strongly supports DBA combination with mixed weighting to leverage both the trend-champion svr and seasonality-champion ONLY_FT_catboost while accounting for significant model diversity."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}, "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": true,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=4 indicates per-horizon selection is appropriate. relative_spread_mean=145.78 shows extreme model disagreement, favoring dba_combination (recommended_method_hint). RMSE spread ratio=1.9073 (>30% gap) justifies inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for mixed trend/seasonality patterns. top_k=3 chosen due to n_windows=3 constraint."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The true series exhibits mixed trend directions (up/down/up) with strong seasonal patterns. Models show extreme disagreement in seasonality capture (variance 0.942) and high overall model disagreement. The data strongly supports DBA combination with mixed weighting to leverage both the trend-champion svr and seasonality-champion ONLY_FT_catboost while accounting for significant model diversity.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=4 indicates per-horizon selection is appropriate. relative_spread_mean=145.78 shows extreme model disagreement, favoring dba_combination (recommended_method_hint). RMSE spread ratio=1.9073 (>30% gap) justifies inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for mixed trend/seasonality patterns. top_k=3 chosen due to n_windows=3 constraint.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - baselines never leak, selection uses validation data, and weighted candidate with n_windows=3 in expanding mode is safe. Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). Universe leaderboards show no single candidate beats all current candidates across all metrics. Current set is optimal.
